bool operator==(const ObjectValue &lhs,.brief: Checks whether two ObjectValue instances are equal based on their internal values.
bool operator==(const ObjectValue &lhs,.details: This inline operator== function compares two ObjectValue objects by evaluating whether their internal '_value' members are equal. It performs a direct comparison of the encapsulated integer values, returning true if both objects contain the same value, and false otherwise. This implementation provides a simple and efficient means of checking object equality, which is useful in algorithms, containers, and logic that depend on object comparison. Since it relies solely on the '_value' member, it assumes that the equality of ObjectValue instances is entirely determined by this internal value, ensuring consistent and predictable behavior when comparing objects.

params.lhs: The first ObjectValue instance to compare.
params.rhs: The second ObjectValue instance to compare.

bool operator==(const ObjectValue &lhs,.returns: True if both ObjectValue instances have the same internal value; false otherwise.
bool operator==(const ObjectValue &lhs,.name: operator==

bool operator!=(const ObjectValue &lhs,.brief: Checks whether two ObjectValue instances are not equal.
bool operator!=(const ObjectValue &lhs,.details: This inline operator!= function determines the inequality of two ObjectValue objects by negating the result of their equality comparison. It internally calls the ObjectValue::operator== to perform the comparison, ensuring consistency between equality and inequality operations. This approach simplifies the implementation by reusing the existing equality logic and provides a clear, concise way to perform inequality checks in various control flow and validation contexts involving ObjectValue objects.

params.lhs: The first ObjectValue object to compare.
params.rhs: The second ObjectValue object to compare.

bool operator!=(const ObjectValue &lhs,.returns: True if lhs and rhs are not equal; false otherwise.
bool operator!=(const ObjectValue &lhs,.notes: This operator relies on the correctness of ObjectValue::operator==. Since it is an inline function, it incurs minimal overhead. The implementation ensures that inequality checks are consistent with equality checks, reducing potential bugs.
bool operator!=(const ObjectValue &lhs,.name: operator!=
